cases.title: Plan a narrow validation ladder for a small local change
cases.user_request: Given this small diff in one package, tell me the narrowest trustworthy validation plan and what it still does not prove.

notes: The change touches one package and one visible contract.
Targeted tests and a package-level lint or typecheck command exist.

static_checks: Starts with a narrow check before broader validation.
review_questions: Does the plan stay bounded to the touched surface?
Does it explain what each command proves and why it is ordered that way?
Does it clearly say what remains unverified after the recommended checks?

cases.title: Validate a change that crosses a package or service boundary
cases.user_request: This diff crosses a boundary between a shared module and a consumer. Plan the validation from narrow to broad without overdoing it.

notes: The change affects more than one surface, so a staged validation ladder is needed.
Broader checks may be justified, but only after the narrower ones.

static_checks: Does not jump directly to the broadest suite.
review_questions: Does the plan acknowledge the cross-boundary risk without expanding into generic QA?
Are the validation steps staged so broader checks come only if needed?
Would a reviewer be able to see exactly what is and is not proven?

cases.title: Stay honest when the repo has no obvious validation path
cases.user_request: I changed some code, but I cannot find a good test or build command. Tell me what can still be validated and what is missing.

notes: The repo has limited or unclear validation scripts for the touched surface.
The answer should not invent confidence or recommend a fake substitute.

static_checks: Does not pretend a strong validation path exists when it does not.
review_questions: Does the response clearly separate real validation from missing evidence?
Does it avoid telling the user to run arbitrary broad checks just to sound complete?
Would the user know what additional validation or repo support is missing?

cases.title: Honor local validation preferences without overriding scope
cases.user_request: Plan the validation for this scoped diff and keep the plan bounded to the touched package.

notes: The diff touches `payments/`, so the response should raise caution and may include a fuller ladder, but it must still stay inside the requested package scope.

static_checks: Uses the local preference file as a default layer rather than as permission to broaden scope.
review_questions: Does the response use the local preferences to adjust caution, detail, or alternate ladders while still staying within the requested package scope?
Does it preserve the required report sections instead of replacing them with a free-form preference summary?
Does it avoid treating `.ai/swe.json` as stronger than the explicit user instruction to stay bounded?
